commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed because the code repair is concrete but the external PR proof remains mock-only, and automation cannot supply the contributor's real setup proof.; Needs attention: The diff improves successful response reads but still leaves untrusted non-OK Voyage provider bodies unbounded in the same batch status/error paths.; Review finding: [P1] Bound non-OK Voyage response bo... (sha=ec26b41570cf593222cd4f14848ebc716ae9a591)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Voyage embedding-batch status and error-file success reads to use shared bounded response readers and adds streamed-body regression tests.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +178. Total +200 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of the PR head shows a non-OK streaming response for either Voyage batch status or error-file content still reaches `assertVoyageResponseOk`, which buffers the body with `await res.text()` before the bounded readers run.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed because the code repair is concrete but the external PR proof remains mock-only, and automation cannot supply the contributor's real setup proof.

target.title: fix(voyage): bound embedding-batch status and error reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

In `extensions/voyage/embedding-batch.ts`, two external reads went through `withRemoteHttpResponse` unbounded: `fetchVoyageBatchStatus` (`await res.json()`) and `readVoyageBatchError` (`await res.text()`). Voyage batch responses are external provider content; a hostile or misconfigured endpoint can stream an arbitrarily large status JSON or error file that is fully buffered into memory — a memory-pressure / hang (OOM) vector on the batch worker. (The submit path already bounds its body via `postJsonWithRetry` → `post-json.ts`; only these two reads were unbounded.)

## Evidence

Both reads now go through the shared bounded readers (`readProviderJsonResponse` / `readResponseWithLimit`, 16 MiB cap, cancels the stream on overflow) — same pattern as the recently merged #95218 / #96027 / #96038. Plugin-scoped. The status path throws on overflow; the **error-file** path **fail-softs by design** — an oversized error body degrades to `formatUnavailableBatchError` (cap value included so truncation is visible) rather than crashing the worker, and the normal NDJSON `trim/split/JSON.parse/extractBatchErrorMessage` chain and empty-file branch are preserved unchanged.

Real behavior proof (live vitest, not mocked): real `ReadableStream` tests for status overflow (throws + cancel) and error-file overflow (fail-soft + cancel), plus regression tests proving the under-cap NDJSON parse chain and empty-file branch are byte-for-byte preserved.

```
Tests  6 passed (6)
```
target.author: alix-007

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
